title: Fresh Fermented Malaysian Beef Skewers
description: A sugar-free-friendly Malaysian dish featuring lean minced beef, fermented to create a fresh and adventurous flavor profile, served on a robata-grill
instructions: In a large bowl, combine minced beef, fish sauce, lime juice, grated ginger, garlic, chili flakes, salt, and black pepper, and mix well with your hands until just combined.
Cover the bowl with plastic wrap and refrigerate for at least 2 hours or overnight to allow the flavors to meld and the meat to ferment slightly.
Preheat the robata-grill to medium-high heat, about 400 degrees Fahrenheit.
Thread a small amount of the beef mixture onto each bamboo skewer, leaving a small space between each piece of meat.
Brush the grill with coconut oil to prevent sticking.
Place the skewers on the grill and cook for 8-10 minutes, turning occasionally, until the meat is cooked through and slightly charred.
Serve the skewers hot, garnished with sliced fresh cilantro and lime wedges.
Allow the skewers to rest for a few minutes before serving to allow the juices to redistribute.
Cook ground beef to an internal temperature of 71°C / 160°F.
